properties.displayName: Enforce Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) security best practices
properties.description: This initiative enforces security best practices for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters, including network configuration, authentication, and add-on policies. By implementing these policies, you can enhance the security posture of your AKS clusters and ensure compliance with organizational standards.

nonComplianceMessages.message: AKS clusters must meet Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements. See guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must use Azure CNI with Overlay mode to improve scalability and reduce IP exhaustion risk. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Azure Container Network Interface (CNI) with Overlay Mode

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must have Microsoft Entra ID integration enabled for identity-based cluster access control. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Microsoft Entra ID Integration

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable Kubernetes RBAC (Kubernetes-native authorization) using Kubernetes Role and RoleBinding objects. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable Azure RBAC for Kubernetes authorization, using Azure role assignments for Microsoft Entra identities. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Azure RBAC (Azure Role Assignments)

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must have local authentication methods disabled. When this policy is set to Deny, cluster create or update operations are blocked if local authentication is enabled. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Require AKS Local Authentication Methods Disabled

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable Workload Identity so pods can use federated credentials instead of stored secrets. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must use managed identity (system-assigned or user-assigned) instead of service principal credentials. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable the OIDC issuer endpoint as a prerequisite for Workload Identity federation. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable the Secrets Store CSI Driver add-on to integrate secret retrieval from Azure Key Vault. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Secrets Store Container Storage Interface (CSI) Driver

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must enable Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS) security features for network policy enforcement and threat detection. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Enforce AKS Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS) Security

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters must use the Cilium dataplane for advanced eBPF-based networking and policy enforcement. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/

Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters should have the Azure Policy Add-on installed and enabled to support policy governance in-cluster. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Audit AKS Azure Policy Add-on Installation

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: Deploy the Azure Policy Add-on to AKS clusters that do not already have it to establish policy enforcement controls. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Deploy Azure Policy Add-on to Azure Kubernetes Service Clusters

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: Deploy Image Cleaner to AKS clusters to reduce risk from stale or vulnerable container images. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
nonComplianceMessages.policyDefinitionReferenceId: Deploy Image Cleaner on Azure Kubernetes Service Clusters

nonComplianceMessages.message: Azure Landing Zone AKS security requirements: AKS clusters should enable Image Cleaner to continuously remove stale or vulnerable images. Guidance: https://developer.gov.bc.ca/docs/default/component/public-cloud-techdocs/azure/azure-services/aks/
